reasoning: To resolve positively, there must be over 311 ACLED-recorded 'Protests' in Romania in a single 30-day window. This is roughly 10 times the monthly historical average (which recorded ~362 total protests over the entire chaotic preceding year). Romania recently experienced profound political instability and a cancelled presidential election between December 2024 and May 2025, but even during this peak unrest, there were nowhere near 311 protest events per month (with specific ACLED reports noting around 50 far-right demonstrations total across several months of this crisis). Achieving >311 protests in ONE month would essentially require almost an entire year's worth of demonstrations completely condensed into 4 weeks. Since no events of such an astronomical magnitude are currently underway for the September periods, and because doing so would require an extreme, historically unprecedented nationwide civil mobilization in the future, the probabilities are fundamentally anchored effectively at the lowest permitted bounds across all timeframes. Uncertainty naturally increases to slightly higher single digits for 2026.
